case_title: ROBERT COLLINS et al VS. EUGENE THOMAS HOWELL et al

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT HOWELL, EUGENE THOMAS INDIVIDUALLY
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT HOWELL, EUGENE THOMAS INDIVIDUALLY JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS
CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT HOWELL, EUGENE THOMAS INDIVIDUALLY AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT COLLINS, ROBERT INDIVIDUALLY ROES 1 TO 10

SANCTIONS ORDERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00 AGAINST AARON FOWLER, FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR AT THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING ON: AUG-05-2004 PAYABLE ON OR BEFORE AUG-20-2004. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: RHONDA AQUILINA. (212) NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA AARON FOWLER'S COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED BY COURT AT ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING SEP-02-2004 AT 1:30PM. THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE FILE FINDS NO GOOD CAUSE OR SUBSTANCIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR PLAINTIFF AARON FOWLER'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS AND RULES. THE COURT FINDS THAT A LESS SEVERE SANCTION WOULD NOT BE EFFECTIVE DUE TO THE HISTORY OF LACK OF COMPLIANCE. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: MARGARET ASHWELL, CSR#7578. (212) NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINUTES FOR JAN-03-2005 3:30 PM FOR DEPT 606

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS -- THOMAS FEENEY APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. NO ARGUMENT WAS MADE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY, ISSUE OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. UNLESS A PROPERLY EXECUTED AUTHORIZATION FOR RELEASE OF MEDICAL RECORDS FROM THE VETERAN'S ADMINISTRATION IS PROVIDED TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT BY 5:00 P.M. ON JANUARY 7, 2005, PLAINTIFF IS PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE AT TRIAL OF ANY INJURY TO HIS KNEES AND / OR THE CONDITION OF HIS KNEES, EITHER BEFORE OR AFTER THE MOTOR VEHICLE ACCIDENT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS ACTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT FAILURE TO EXECUTE SAID AUTHORIZATION IS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AS PLAINTIFF HAS PREVIOUSLY AGREED TO PROVIDE SAID AUTHORIZATION, TESTIFIED THAT THE INCIDENT AGGRAVATED A PRE-EXISTING CONDITION, AND RECEIVED TREATMENT FOR SUCH CONDITION AT VETERAN'S ADMINISTRATION MEDICAL FACILITIES IN BOTH SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA AND SHREVEPORT, LOUISIANA. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT DISMISS WITH PREJUDICE THE COMPLAINT OF ROBERT COLLINS. EACH SIDE TO BEAR THEIR OWN COSTS.

MINI MINUTES: Melvin S. Hodges, Esq., specially appearing for Plaintiff Robert Collins. Appearance for Defendant Eugene Thomas Howell. Court ordered Plaintiff's Aaron Fowler's Complaint Dismissed. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substancial justification for plaintiff Aaron Fowler's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, It is hereby ordered that Complaint filed by defendant in Propria Persona Aaron Fowler is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Court ordered the Arbitrator's Jurisdiction extended to 12/22/2004. The Arbitration compliance OSC is re-set to 1/20/2005 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. Proceedings reported by: Margaret Ashwell, CSR#7578. (212)

OSC is ordered re-set to 9/2/2004 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212. OSC to Plaintiff in Propria Persona Aaron Fowler only.
